title: Chapter 3 - Your First Repo: Status, Add, Commit
description: Check your understanding of staging and committing changes.

questions.prompt: What does `git status` help you see?

options.label: Which files have changed and are staged or unstaged
A list of all programming languages

explanations.overview: `git status` tells you what changed and what is ready to commit.

byOptionId.a: Not quite. Git does not track weather.
byOptionId.b: Correct. It shows changed files and their staging state.
byOptionId.c: Not quite. GitHub followers are unrelated to Git status.
byOptionId.d: Not quite. Git status is about your repository state.

questions.prompt: What is the staging area used for?

options.label: Choosing which changes go into the next commit

explanations.overview: Staging is the selection step before committing.

byOptionId.a: Not quite. Staging is about selecting changes, not uploading.
byOptionId.b: Not quite. Staging does not delete history.
byOptionId.c: Not quite. Staging does not rename repositories.
byOptionId.d: Correct. Staging lets you pick what to include in the next commit.

options.label: Stages changes so they can be committed
Writes a commit message for you
Deletes a file from your computer

explanations.overview: `git add` stages changes for the next commit.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. Creating a GitHub repo is a separate step.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. You write commit messages when you commit.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. `git add` does not delete files.

questions.prompt: Why would you use a `.gitignore` file?

options.label: To speed up your internet connection
To force Git to track every file
To ignore files you do not want to track in Git

explanations.overview: `.gitignore` helps prevent committing temporary files, build output, and secrets.

byOptionId.a: Not quite. `.gitignore` does not change your internet speed.
byOptionId.b: Not quite. It does not make commits anonymous.
byOptionId.c: Not quite. It does the opposite by telling Git to ignore certain files.
byOptionId.d: Correct. `.gitignore` tells Git what not to track.
